results.title: Parallel lines
results.creator_credit: Larry Nelson, High MacDonald and New Zealand National Film Unit
results.brief_description: An animator’s view of the evolution of life in New Zealand from the earth’s beginnings through to the arrival of the first humans - the Maoris. The art technique used builds on the fluid metamorphosis of parallel lines.

results.title: Olympic Games, 1960, Rome = La Grande olimpiade
results.creator_credit: Romolo Marcellini, Cineriz and Realiegato dall'Institute Nationale
results.brief_description: After the arrival of the Olympic torch from Greece, and a short history of the Games, Pope John welcomes the athletes and the Games are opened in an impressive ceremony. Interspersed between the various events are glimpses of the tourists and competitors. Includes Herb Elliott’s world record and olympic record, Dawn Fraser’s world record and Australia winning the cross-country riding event. The final highlights are the great marathon and the closing ceremony.

results.title: Grove music
results.brief_description: The Grove - better known as London’s Notting Hill Gate district associated with a history of black militancy. But amid the violence has emerged a music which gives expression to the culture and politics of black youth. Bands appearing in this film include: Inity, Aswad, Sons of Jah, Brimstone and Junior Brown.

results.title: A Curious and diverse flora. Parts 1-5
results.creator_credit: Tony Chenn, Nicholas Alexander, Australian Academy of Science and CSIRO
results.brief_description: Australian flora is shown as first discovered by Europeans in Botany Bay. Other Australian flora is shown from the isolated area of South Western Australia and from Tasmania. The distinctive flora of eucalypt forests and tropical rainforests is also shown. The program is in five parts. Written and presented by Dr. Peter Valder. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: Ten commandments of love
results.brief_description: A photographic still image of two people in an impassioned embrace on an ocean pier. Harvey and the Moonglows start singing ‘The ten commandments of love’ to a background of changing color.

results.title: Language of the deaf
results.brief_description: Explores the many ways in which deaf people enjoy all facets of life-from rock concerts to playing football - and deals with the social implications of being deaf. Highlighted is the role of Gallaudet College in Washington D.C., the only institution of higher learning in the world for the deaf.

results.title: The Pale horseman
results.creator_credit: Irving Jacoby and United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration
results.brief_description: Depicts health problems that follow in the wake of war, and explains how hunger and exposure have caused the spread of tuberculosis, cholera and malaria. Shows what Army doctors and UNRRA scientists have done in liberated countries to prevent the spread of epidemics.

results.title: Ah...we humans
results.brief_description: A non-narrated, animated cartoon with humorous sound effects, giving an understanding of how people react when confronted with behaviour patterns different from their own. Deals with a number of concepts related to groups and conflict: cultural awareness, beliefs and ignorance, scarce resources and the value of alliances. No other credits available.
